data.title: As a test-writing agent, I know whether a task requires RED, GREEN, or UPDATE testing so I write the right kind of test
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Update the implementation-plan skill to classify each task's testing mode: RED (behavior changes requiring new failing tests), GREEN (implementation changes without behavior change requiring only green validation), or UPDATE (behavior changes with existing test coverage requiring test updates).
**Why**: Currently implementation-plan does not distinguish between tasks that change behavior and tasks that change implementation, causing unnecessary test creation and wasted agent effort.
**Scope**:
- IN: Add testing_mode field (RED/GREEN/UPDATE) to implementation-plan task output format
- IN: Frame testing tasks in terms of behavior rather than mechanics
- IN: Classification logic based on whether the task changes observable behavior and whether existing tests cover it
- OUT: Sprint orchestrator routing changes (separate story)
- OUT: Modifying the shared testing standard itself

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, implementation-plan emits a testing_mode field (RED, GREEN, or UPDATE) for each task in its output
  ← Satisfies: "Implementation-plan classifies each task's testing mode: RED, GREEN, or UPDATE"
- When this story is complete, implementation-plan frames testing tasks in terms of behavior rather than mechanics
  ← Satisfies: "Implementation-plan frames testing tasks in terms of behavior rather than mechanics"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic
  ← Satisfies: TDD done-of-done

## Considerations

- [Testing] testing_mode is a new LLM classification decision — eval coverage for RED/GREEN/UPDATE edge cases needed
- [Maintainability] Task output format change must be backward-compatible for consumers that don't yet read testing_mode

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
